back_translation: Jesus said to his disciples, "Things that cause one to go astray will certainly come unavoidably; but woe to the person who causes such things to happen.
It would still be better for that person to have a millstone placed around his neck and be thrown into the sea, than that he should cause one of these little ones to go astray.
You (plural) watch yourselves carefully. If your brother sins, admonish him; and if he repents, forgive him.
Even if he sins against you seven times in a single day, and returns to you seven times saying, 'I have repented,' you must forgive him."
The apostles said to the Lord, "Please increase our faith for us."
The Lord said, "If you have faith as small as one mustard seed, you could command this mulberry tree, 'Be uprooted and be planted in the sea,' and the tree would obey you.
Which of you, having a servant who is plowing or tending sheep, when that servant comes back in from the field, would say to him, 'Come recline at the table and eat immediately'?
But would not the master say to him, 'Prepare my evening meal, gird yourself and serve me until I have finished eating and drinking, and after that you may eat and drink'?
Would the master thank the servant because he did what was commanded?
In the same way, you also, when you have done all the things commanded of you, should say, 'We are servants not deserving of praise; we have only done that which was our duty to do.'"
As Jesus journeyed toward Jerusalem, he was passing along the border between the region of Samaria and the region of Galilee.
When he entered a certain village, ten leprous men came out to meet him. They stood at a distance
and called out in a loud voice, "Jesus, Master, please have mercy on us."
When he saw them, he said, "Go and show yourselves to the priests." And as they were going, they were cleansed of the leprosy.
But one of them, when he saw that he had been healed from the disease, came back to him praising God in a loud voice,
and bowed face-down at the feet of Jesus to thank him. This man was a Samaritan.
Jesus said, "Were not all ten cleansed? Then where are the other nine?
Did no one return to give glory to God except this foreigner only?"
Then he said to that man, "Rise and go. Your faith has saved you."
When the Pharisees asked him, "When will the kingdom of God come?" Jesus answered them, "The kingdom of God does not come in a form that can be observed,
nor will people say, 'Look, it is here' or 'It is there.' For behold, the kingdom of God is already among you."
Then he said to the disciples, "Days will come when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not see it.
People will tell you, 'Look, it is there' or 'Look, it is here.' Do not go out and do not run after them.
For just as the lightning flashes and shines from one part of the sky to the other, so will the Son of Man be in his day.
But first he must suffer many things and be rejected by the people of this generation.
And as it was in the days of Noah, so also will it be in the days of the Son of Man.
People were eating and drinking, marrying and giving daughters in marriage, until the day Noah entered into the large boat. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.
Likewise, what happened in the days of Lot was just the same. People were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting trees and building houses.
But on the day Lot left the city of Sodom, fire and sulfur fell from the sky like rain and destroyed them all.
On the day that the Son of Man reveals himself, it will be just like that.

On that day, the one who is on the roof of the house, and has his possessions inside the house, must not go down to fetch them. And the one in the field likewise must not turn back to retrieve what he has left behind.
Remember Lot's wife.
Whoever tries to preserve his life will lose that life, and whoever is willing to lose his life will preserve that life.
I tell you that on that night, there will be two people sleeping on the same bed; one will be taken away, but the other will be left behind.
There will be two women grinding grain together; one will be taken away, but the other will be left behind."
The disciples asked him, "Lord, where will it happen?" He said to them, "Wherever there is a carcass, there the vultures will gather."
